hypothesis: Systematic differences in GFN2-xTB relaxation energy between flow-matching and diffusion-based models reflect fundamentally different learned energy landscape representations, such that models trained with a GFN2-xTB-based geometry loss term explicitly penalizing bond length, bond angle, and torsion deviations during training—rather than only at evaluation—will close the observed performance gap between flow-matching and diffusion architectures by at least 50% without requiring increased model capacity or additional inference steps.
